page: Microsoft Azure E64ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 32 physical cores with hyper-threading. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The CPU operates at 4.2 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations.

The instance includes 3780 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. In benchmark evaluations, the E64ds_v7 demonstrates strong performance, placing in the top 25% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests. Its memory subsystem achieves strong read-only bandwidth, reaching 5421.5 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 332.26 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression both rank in the top 10% tier, highlighting high single-core efficiency.

This hardware profile is highly optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations, supported by a Passmark database score in the top 10% and Redis SET performance of 117.01 million operations per second. The inclusion of Intel AMX instructions enables efficient CPU-based machine learning, reflected in top-tier prompt processing speeds for large 70B LLM models. Given its high resource density, dedicated CPU allocation, and substantial bundled local storage, the server offers a qualitatively cost-effective platform for enterprise databases, large-scale caching layers, and memory-bound analytical workloads without requiring dedicated GPU hardware.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz, it features 32 physical cores, a 480 MB L3 cache, and support for Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions. The server includes 3780 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in database operations, single-threaded compression, and static web serving, alongside strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth results. Lacking a GPU, it leverages CPU-based acceleration for workloads like LLM prompt processing. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, enterprise databases, and large-scale data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64ds_v7 is an Edsv7 VM with 64 Intel vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, designed for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-memory, x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and substantial local storage for demanding database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz with 480 MB L3 cache
512.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio
3780 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support accelerating CPU-based vector and matrix operations
Top-tier benchmark performance in database operations and static web serving
Well-suited for enterprise databases, large-scale caching, and memory-bound analytics
